paths.title: Learn ASL
paths.tagline: Bite-sized units across every part of daily ASL.

units.description: Your first signs, greetings, and introductions.

steps.title: Your Turn
steps.prompt: Record yourself signing, then compare with the example.
I and me use the same sign — point to yourself. English uses two words; ASL uses one.
We and us share one sign — point toward your group in space.
Your and yours share one possessive sign toward the other person.
Fill the blank in the sentence.
My and mine are the same possessive sign toward yourself.

He and him are the same sign — point to where you set up that person.
She and her share one sign — point back to her spot in space.
They and them use the same sign — point to that group in space.
